content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Amendment fr-2-eb6bf34a is technically correct. The test at lines 4153-4177 spawns a real 'sleep 300' child and sends SIGKILL, which the OS reaps nearly instantly. ManagedChild::wait() calls try_wait() on its first iteration (line 494), finds the process already reaped, and returns immediately — well within the 100ms timeout. The weak-ref assertion (line 4174) is path-insensitive: both the happy path and the timeout path drop the Arc, so it passes regardless of which branch executed. The tracing::warn! at lines 2014-2018, which is the core safety feature added by this change, has zero deterministic test coverage. A mock or harness that forces wait() to remain pending past the deadline is needed to properly validate the timeout branch.

## Votes

- `fr-2-eb6bf34a`: Accept
  Verified by reading the code: (1) spawn_background_reap_with_timeout wraps force_kill()+wait() in tokio::time::timeout at line 2013. (2) ManagedChild::wait() polls try_wait() immediately at line 494; after SIGKILL on a normal 'sleep 300' process, the OS reaps it within microseconds, so wait() returns on the first poll — far below the 100ms timeout. (3) The test's weak-ref assertion at line 4174 passes whether the normal path or timeout path executed, since both drop the Arc. This means the timeout/warning branch (lines 2013-2018) — the primary new safety feature — is never actually exercised. The test provides false confidence. A deterministic harness that forces wait() to block past the deadline is the correct fix to get real coverage of the timeout warning path.
